title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, Social Media Platform for AI Bots
summary: Meta has acquired Moltbook, a social media platform for AI bots, with plans to develop new ways for AI agents to work for people and businesses.

title: UK Government Bans Al Quds Day March Over Public Disorder Fears
summary: The UK government has banned the annual Al Quds Day march in London due to concerns over public disorder, with a stationary demonstration to be held instead under strict conditions.

title: Epstein's Aides Still Control His Money and Secrets, Despite Congressional Subpoena
summary: Richard Kahn and Darren Indyke, the sole executors of Jeffrey Epstein's estate, are still controlling his wealth and possessions, despite being subpoenaed to testify before a congressional committee.

title: Former Deputy PM Clegg Criticizes University Tuition Fee System
summary: Former Deputy PM Sir Nick Clegg has criticized the university tuition fee system, describing it as "deeply unfair" due to the repeated freezing of graduate repayment thresholds.

title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: Family Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Failure to Warn Authorities
summary: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI for allegedly failing to warn authorities about the suspect's plans.

title: FAA Briefly Grounds JetBlue Flights Due to System Outage
summary: The Federal Aviation Administration briefly grounded all JetBlue flights due to a system outage, which was resolved within 40 minutes. The airline did not provide further information on the cause of t
